events.text: Nancy Robinson, 38-year-old Asian female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Malignant melanoma of the Left upper back. Clinical staging: T3bN2aM1c, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C43.9. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent primary resection: Wide local excision with 2 cm margins and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 6 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy: Dabrafenib 150 mg BID + Trametinib 2 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Completed brain metastases treatment: Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) 20 Gy single fraction to 2 brain metastases. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 immune-related colitis. Prednisone 1 mg/kg initiated. Colonoscopy showed mild colitis. Immunotherapy held.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after alternative first-line immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after alternative first-line immunotherapy.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Date of Study: 2023-08-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.3 cm in the right lower lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Specimen ID: S2023-11524
Date of Procedure: 2023-08-14
Date of Report: 2023-08-16

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.5 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Nancy Robinson - Left upper back" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 3.5 x 2.4 x 1.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Dermatology

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Date: 2023-08-20
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Robinson is a 38-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed malignant melanoma of the left upper back, IV. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases. Pathology confirmed malignant melanoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- atrial fibrillation
- hypertension
- coronary artery disease

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (0.5 ppd)
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: Codeine (nausea)

MEDICATIONS:
- Apixaban 5 mg BID
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent nausea, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 145/72, HR 76, RR 18, Temp 98.3F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 63.5 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.9, Hgb 10.6, Plt 316, Cr 1.06

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
38-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. initial oncology consultation.

1. Malignant melanoma of the left upper back, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Dermatology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Date of Report: 2023-08-21
Specimen: Left upper back (Resection)
Diagnosis: Malignant melanoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 19.0 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRAF p.V600E (c.1799T>A) - Missense (Exon 15) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion
  TERT Promoter mutation (-124C>T) - Promoter

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  NF1 p.T1556M - VUS
  ARID2 p.Q1334L - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRAF V600E: FDA-approved BRAF/MEK inhibitors include dabrafenib/trametinib, encorafenib/binimetinib, vemurafenib/cobimetinib.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 736x coverage.
Tumor content: 44%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Specimen ID: S2023-97113
Date of Procedure: 2023-09-14
Date of Report: 2023-09-17

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Nancy Robinson - Left upper back" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 2.8 x 2.0 x 1.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Date: 2023-09-19
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Robinson is a 38-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports feeling well overall. Weight is stable.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: unintentional weight loss, abdominal bloating, joint pain, constipation. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 141/67, HR 66, RR 19, Temp 98.2F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 95.7 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.8, Hgb 9.3, Plt 214, Cr 1.15

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
38-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. postoperative follow-up.

1. Malignant melanoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Fatigue management discussed
3. Follow-up in 2 weeks

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

FINDINGS:

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

BRAIN MRI WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Nancy Robinson
MRN: patient_d8519ce72d5c
Date of Study: 2024-10-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 7 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
BRAIN: 2 enhancing lesions identified: right temporal lobe (0.8 cm), left parietal lobe (1.6 cm). Surrounding vasogenic edema. No midline shift.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.
